steps.title: Classify the model
steps.content: Desmos gives $a=20000$ and $b=1.13$. Since the variable is in the exponent, the model is exponential; since $1.13>1$, it is increasing.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The model is increasing exponential, so choice B is correct.

steps.explanation:
• Choice A: Decreasing exponential — would require a base between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.87). Eliminated.
  • Choice B: Increasing exponential — base is 1.13 > 1 and grows each year. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: Decreasing linear — a constant percentage increase is not linear and does not decrease. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: Increasing linear — linear means adding the same amount each year, not the same percentage. 13% of a growing base adds more each year, making it exponential. Eliminated.
